description: Actenon strict JSON canonicalisation profile. A strict subset of RFC 8785 (JCS) that rejects floating-point values entirely instead of canonicalising them. All signed and digested cross-repo artifacts use this profile.

normative_rules.object_keys: string only; non-string keys are rejected
normative_rules.key_ordering: lexicographic byte order (sorted by Unicode code point)
normative_rules.integer_support: full; serialized as decimal digits with optional leading minus
normative_rules.float_support: prohibited; any float value raises TypeError before canonicalisation
normative_rules.nan_support: prohibited; NaN is rejected before canonicalisation
normative_rules.infinity_support: prohibited; +Infinity and -Infinity are rejected before canonicalisation
normative_rules.list_handling: deterministic; elements canonicalised in order; tuples treated as lists
normative_rules.boolean_handling: true and false are canonical; serialized as lowercase
normative_rules.null_handling: null is canonical; serialized as lowercase
normative_rules.string_handling: RFC 8259 string encoding; ensure_ascii=False (UTF-8 output); no insignificant whitespace
normative_rules.numeric_coercion: none; integers stay integers; no implicit float conversion; no implementation-dependent numeric coercion

legacy_note: The identifier 'RFC8785-JCS' was used by historical proofs. It represented Actenon's strict subset (float rejection) but was mislabelled as full RFC 8785. New proofs use ACTENON-JCS-STRICT-1. Historical proofs with 'RFC8785-JCS' continue to verify under the same canonicalisation logic.
